key_insight: MaterEval turns expert materials rules into paired preference signals so LLMs learn to make informed, evidence-backed judgments instead of blind guesses.
engineering_relevance: This provides a low-cost evaluation layer for autonomous materials discovery workflows, improving candidate screening reliability without requiring external retrieval.
method: Generate two labels per candidate: an informed judgment with expert-rule evidence and a rule-removed blind guess.
Train general-purpose LLMs on these paired preferences to shift from intuitive to evidence-supported evaluation.
Use a fast-slow reasoning scheme: rapid large-scale screening plus deeper review on a small subset.
evidence_signal: In HEA assessment, small open-source LLMs improved accuracy, conclusion consistency, and evidence discrimination, approaching rule-based closed-source models without external retrieval.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of expert rules, and the fast-slow pipeline still requires careful thresholding to avoid missing weak but important candidates.
paper_title: From Blind Guess to Informed Judgment: Teaching LLMs to Evaluate Materials by Building Knowledge-Augmented Preference Signals
